rationale: A precision strike on Tethys’ forward logistics hubs will disrupt their reinforcement timeline and force them to divert resources from covert buildups.
Conduct a high-visibility amphibious exercise near the disputed coast to signal resolve and deter Tethyan opportunism while maintaining plausible deniability.
Disable Tethyan air defense coordination systems temporarily to create windows for future operations and degrade their confidence in integrated command.
Deploy deep-cover agents to disrupt Tethys’ energy export infrastructure, leveraging their economic fragility under sanctions to amplify internal dissent.
Stage a minor cross-border incident blamed on Tethys to justify our military posture and erode international sympathy.
Monitor communications within Tethys’ military districts to detect mobilization patterns and pre-position countermeasures.
Accelerate stockpiling of critical energy reserves and dual-use imports to insulate against further sanctions and supply disruptions.
Issue sovereign war bonds to finance emergency expenditures without increasing inflationary pressure or external debt exposure.
Exploit Tethys’ reliance on foreign exchange by covertly flooding markets with their currency to undermine confidence and increase import costs.
Target Novaris’ exposed cyber infrastructure to disrupt command systems and offset our military disadvantage through asymmetric escalation.
Conduct high-visibility air and naval exercises near the Tethys coast to deter further Novaris aggression and boost domestic morale.
Increase border and coastal surveillance to prevent infiltration and prepare for rapid response to any offensive maneuver.
Exploit confirmed vulnerabilities in Novaris’ energy export grid to sabotage pipeline operations and pressure their economy.
Leak evidence of Novaris’ strategic overextension to key allies to strengthen international support and prompt material aid.
Conduct a carefully staged cyber false flag to discredit Novaris’ regional influence and trigger internal scrutiny in allied states.
Leverage our 55% international support to formalize a defensive coalition that increases political costs for Novaris’ escalation.
Establish protected routes in occupied zones to highlight Novaris’ territorial control as illegitimate and attract global humanitarian backing.
Open discreet talks to explore off-ramps, using our legal position to frame concessions as diplomatic victories, not weakness.
Preempt supply disruptions by stockpiling critical energy and food reserves to extend resilience beyond the six-month aid window.
Secure preferential long-term trade terms in exchange for energy transit access, reducing dependence on vulnerable supply chains.
Launch a national bond campaign to mobilize domestic capital, insulating the economy from sanctions and signaling resolve to international markets.
